Live Workshop · Day 2
Purely Personal · 18 July 2026

Put your brain online.

Yesterday you built your second brain on your laptop. Today it goes live on the internet as your own AI Second Brain. It answers in your voice, writes in your brand, and produces content on demand through the AI CMO department, all from one dashboard, reachable from any device.

5 stages
~45 minutes
Only 2 accounts needed
Zero code, one deploy click

What today turns on

Day 1 built the brain. Day 2 gives it a front door, a face, and a voice. Your markdown vault becomes your live AI Second Brain: a chat with your AI CEO, a live view of the AI CMO department running your request, a graph of your uploaded brain, and a full Brand Kit, all on one dashboard. Your brand colors, fonts, and tone get locked in once, and then every piece of content it writes uses them automatically.

GitHub

Where the app lives as code. You copy our repository into your own account so the deploy button has something to build from.

The host

Turns that code into a real website with a link. One click, one key pasted, and your brain is live on the internet.

Brain + Brand Kit

Your Day 1 folder loaded in, then your colors, fonts, and voice added once. Together they ground every answer and every piece of content.

The Day 2 route, at a glance

2 accounts → deploy → upload → brand kit → create
0
Before today
Two accounts

GitHub login and an OpenAI API key

1
~10 min
Deploy

Copy the repo, one click, paste your key

2
~10 min
Upload brain

Zip the folder, drop it in

3

Brand Kit

Colors, fonts, voice, identity

4
Live
Create

Posts, carousels, newsletters, images

The one-line version

Copy our repo into your GitHub, click Deploy, add storage with one click, paste your OpenAI key, zip your Day 1 folder, upload it under Second Brain, then fill in your Brand Kit. That is the whole day. Everything below walks through those moves with a screenshot for each click.

Ready when you are

Deploy Your Second Brain

One repo, one click, one key. This is the exact link the whole workshop uses.

Deploy Your Second Brain →

Two things, before anything else

Day 2 needs exactly two accounts. Get these sorted first and the rest is clicking. If you already have both from a previous session, jump straight to Deploy the Repo.

Account 1

A GitHub account

This is where our app gets copied so you own your own private copy. It is free, and you only need the login.

  1. Go to github.com/signup
  2. Enter your email, pick a password, and choose a username
  3. Open the verification email from GitHub and click the link inside
  4. Already have an account? Just log in at github.com/login instead
DONE →You can see your GitHub dashboard when logged in. That is all you need here.
Account 2

An OpenAI API key

This is the fuel. It is what lets your live brain think and write. You paste it once during deploy and never touch it again.

  1. Go to platform.openai.com/api-keys and sign in
  2. Click Create new secret key
  3. Give it a name, for example second-brain
  4. Copy the key the moment it appears on screen
  5. Paste it into your Notes app for a few minutes. You will need it during deploy
GUARD ITYour API key works like a credit card. Keep it private, and only paste it into the one deploy box shown in Stage 2. If anyone else gets it, they can spend on your account.
You need

Just these two

A GitHub login and an OpenAI API key. That is the entire prerequisite list for going live.

Leave for later

Yesterday's tools

The deploy itself runs in your browser, so you can set it up on any computer.

Keep from Day 1

Have your Second-Brain folder from yesterday ready on your desktop. It holds your voice-dna, business-context, and wiki files. You will zip and upload it in Stage 3. If you also have a logo, a founder photo, or brand colors picked out, keep those handy too. You will add them in Stage 4.

Copy the repo, then deploy it

This is the heart of Day 2. You will copy our app into your own GitHub, then hand it to the host with one click. The only thing you type is your API key. Each step below matches one screenshot, so follow them in order.

Stage 2 · Step 1
Step 1 · Open the repo

Go to the shared repository

This is the app we built for you. Everything the live brain needs is already inside it, so you just need your own copy.

  1. Make sure you are logged into GitHub first
  2. Open github.com/danialhussin71/Second-Brain-Workshop
  3. You will see folders like content and src. Leave them as they are
  4. Scroll down to the README, which is where the deploy button lives
Stage 2 · Step 2
Step 2 · Hit deploy

Click the deploy button

Scroll through the README until the deploy button appears. Clicking it starts a New Project screen: it clones the repo into your GitHub account, then walks you through adding storage and your key, a couple of clicks each.

  1. Find the Deploy button inside the README
  2. Click it to begin
  3. When it asks, choose to connect your GitHub, so it clones into your own account
  4. Next you'll add a storage bucket with one click, then paste your key. Both are covered in the next two steps
GOOD →Two things left: add storage with one click, then paste your key. Neither takes more than a few seconds.
Stage 2 · Step 3
Step 3 · Approve GitHub access

Confirm your private copy

You already clicked Deploy in Step 2. This screen is where you confirm the details before the clone actually happens. Nothing here needs to be typed.

  1. Approve the access request on the GitHub screen
  2. Under Git Scope, confirm your own account is selected
  3. Leave the repository name as is
  4. Keep the repository set to Private
Stage 2 · Step 4
Step 4 · Add storage

Click Add next to Blob Store

Scroll down on the same page to Add Products. Your AI Second Brain stores your uploaded vault, brand kit, and assets in a Blob store, and it is one click to attach.

  1. Find the Add Products section, under Storage
  2. Find the Blob Store card in the mandatory products list
  3. Click Add next to it
  4. Leave every setting as it is, nothing here needs configuring
DONE →Storage is attached. Now scroll further down for the one key it asks for.
Stage 2 · Step 5
Step 5 · Paste your key

Add the one secret it asks for

This is the only thing you type in the whole deploy. The field is named OPENAI_API_KEY. Paste your key into the value box, and that is the fuel that makes your AI Second Brain think.

  1. Find the Add Environment Variables screen
  2. Confirm the Name field shows OPENAI_API_KEY
  3. Click the Value box and paste your key from Stage 1
  4. Paste the key on its own, with no label and no quote marks around it
  5. Leave every other field as it is
COMMON SLIPIf the key is missing its first character or has an extra space, the site loads but chat fails with an API-key error. Re-paste the key carefully and that usually fixes it.
Stage 2 · Step 6
Step 6 · Deploy

Press Deploy and wait

Now the host builds your site. This takes about three minutes. You will see build logs scroll past, and a yellow warning or two along the way is normal.

  1. Click the large Deploy button
  2. Watch the Deployment panel show "Deployment started" and then the build logs
  3. Ignore any npm warn deprecated lines, since warnings are safe
  4. Wait about three minutes for the build to finish
Stage 2 · Step 7
Step 7 · You are live

Open your AI Second Brain

When the deploy finishes you land on the deployment page. Your live link sits under Domains, a friendlier address than the one under Deployment. Click it to open your AI Second Brain.

  1. On the deployment page, find the Domains section
  2. Click the domain link (it looks like your-project-name.vercel.app)
  3. Your live AI Second Brain opens in a new tab
  4. Bookmark that domain link, because this is your brain's home on the web
MILESTONEYour Second Brain is now live on the internet. The next stage loads in your own knowledge, and the one after that adds your brand.

Make it your brain

Your live site works the moment it deploys. Now you load it with your own knowledge. You will zip the Day 1 folder, open the Settings panel from your dashboard, and drop the zip into the Second Brain tab.

Stage 3 · Step 1
Step 1 · Compress

Zip your Second-Brain folder

Find the folder you built yesterday. It holds your voice-dna, business-context, and wiki markdown files. You will turn the whole folder into a single .zip.

  1. Find your Second-Brain folder on the desktop
  2. Right-click the folder
  3. Choose Compress "[folder name]" from the menu
DONE →You now have a single .zip file. It contains every markdown file the AI will learn from.
Stage 3 · Step 2
Step 2 · Open settings

Open Knowledge & Settings

On your live dashboard, look for the Settings button in the top-right corner. That opens a single panel for everything about your brain and your brand: Second Brain, Brand Kit, Carousel, and Documents.

  1. Open your live AI Second Brain
  2. Click Settings in the top-right corner
  3. The Knowledge & Settings panel opens on the Second brain tab by default
Stage 3 · Step 3
Step 3 · Upload

Drop your .zip in

This is the moment it becomes yours. The Obsidian vault card takes your .zip directly. Folder paths and [[wiki links]] are retained, so the live graph recreates your knowledge connections.

  1. Under Obsidian vault, click Upload vault
  2. Select the .zip file you compressed in Step 1
  3. Wait for it to process. The note count below the button updates once it is done, for example "55 notes imported"
DONE →Your notes are in. Scroll down and you will see a Knowledge constellation, a live graph of every note and every link between them.

Build a brand your AI can actually use

Your brain knows what you know. The Brand Kit teaches it how you look and sound. Once these fields are filled in, every post, carousel, and image the platform makes uses your colors, your fonts, and your tone automatically, so nothing comes out generic.

Stage 4 · Step 1
Step 1 · Open Brand Kit

Switch to the Brand kit tab

You are still inside the same Knowledge & Settings panel from Stage 3. Click across to Brand kit and you will see a readiness meter and your identity assets.

  1. In the Knowledge & Settings panel, click the Brand kit tab
  2. Check the Brand readiness meter. It tracks how many of the 8 essentials you have filled in
  3. Under Identity assets, click Upload founder face and add a clear photo of yourself
  4. Click Upload logo mark and add your logo
HOW IT FILLS ITSELFThe platform reads everything you already uploaded first, your Day 1 documents included, and fills in what it can find on its own. Where something is missing, like your tone of voice or a color preference, it asks you a short question directly instead of guessing.
Stage 4 · Step 2
Step 2 · Typography, voice, and guardrails

Describe how the brand should read and sound

Scroll down inside the Brand kit tab. These fields are plain text, so write them the way you would describe your brand to a new hire.

  1. Under Typography, name your headline font in Headline system and your body font in Body system
  2. Under Voice DNA, describe the Tone and rhythm your AI CEO should write in
  3. Under Language guardrails, fill in Use more of with the words and habits you want, and Never use with what to avoid
Stage 4 · Step 3
Step 3 · Colour system

Add your exact brand colors

These values are injected directly into every visual the platform generates, so use the real hex codes, not a rough guess. The first color you add becomes the main accent.

  1. Scroll to Colour system
  2. Click into each swatch and enter the hex code for that color
  3. Add the main accent color first, then supporting colors like a dark background, a light background, and a neutral grey
WHERE THESE COME FROMIf you asked your AI CEO about your brand earlier and it gave you back a color palette or font pairing in the chat, that is exactly what belongs here. Copy the hex codes and font names it gave you straight into these fields.

Brand Kit checklist

  • Founder face and logo mark uploaded
  • Headline and body fonts named
  • Voice DNA tone and rhythm described
  • Language guardrails filled in on both sides
  • Colour system has real hex values, accent color first
  • Brand readiness meter close to 100%

Give it one instruction

Your brain knows your business. Your Brand Kit knows your look and your voice. Together they mean you can ask for finished content and get something ready to publish, not a rough draft you have to rewrite.

Stage 5 · The interface
How it's organized

Your AI CEO routes it to the AI CMO department

One instruction goes to your AI CEO. It reads every document in your brain, then hands the job to the AI CMO department, which runs Research and Content underneath it. That's where your instruction becomes a real deliverable.

What you can make

Six content types, one department

Once Second Brain and Brand Kit are both set up, the same instruction box can produce any of these, grounded in your knowledge and styled in your brand.

  • 1Text. Single posts written in your voice, ready to copy and publish.
  • 2Picture. Single-image posts with your logo, colors, and founder photo built in.
  • 3Carousel. Multi-slide posts, using your fonts and colors on every slide.
  • 4Reels. Short-form video scripts, grounded in your business context.
  • 5Long-form. Longer video or article scripts for deeper topics.
  • 6Newsletter. Full email-length pieces, written in your voice.
Stage 5 · Step 1
Step 1 · Ask

Tell the AI CEO what you need

Use plain language and tell it the outcome you want. Name the format if you want a specific one, otherwise the AI CMO department picks the best fit.

  1. Click the main input on your live dashboard
  2. Type a real request, for example post about building your personal brand in 2026 using my voice DNA
  3. Press send, and watch the org chart light up as the request routes through
Stage 5 · Step 2
Step 2 · Receive

Get content in your voice and your brand

The response is grounded in your second brain and styled with your Brand Kit, written the way you would write it because it learned from your own words and your own look.

  1. Read the output on the right, a complete piece grounded in your knowledge
  2. When it shows Run complete · deliverable ready, copy it and publish, or ask for a carousel, newsletter, or image version of the same idea
THAT IS ITYou now have a live AI that knows your business, writes like you, and looks like your brand, reachable from any device. Feed it more knowledge and refine your Brand Kit over time, and it only gets sharper.

Day 2 complete, your live checklist

  • GitHub account created and the repo copied into it
  • Deployed with your OpenAI API key pasted into one field
  • Live link works and opens your Second Brain
  • Your Day 1 folder zipped and uploaded under Second Brain
  • Brand Kit filled in: identity, typography, voice, guardrails, colors
  • You asked for one piece of content and got a deliverable in your voice and brand

When something snags

Almost every snag in Day 2 comes down to the key, the account, the upload, or the Brand Kit. Find your problem below, apply the fix, and keep moving. If none of these match, the rescue prompt at the bottom handles the rest.

Deploy problems

The site is live but chat fails

Error: invalid API key, or chat returns nothing

Cause: the key was pasted with a stray character, or its first letter got clipped. Fix: open your project's Settings, then Environment Variables. Edit the key value, paste your OpenAI key again cleanly, save, and click Redeploy.

Build failed with a red error

Cause: usually a half-finished GitHub connection. Fix: delete the project on the host and run the deploy button again from the repo README. A fresh clone clears it.

A yellow npm warn deprecated line

This is a warning, so it is safe to ignore. Only a red error will stop a deploy.

Forgot to add the Blob Store before deploying

Cause: the storage step was skipped. Fix: open your project on the host, go to Storage, click Add next to Blob Store, then Redeploy so the new storage connects.

Clicked the wrong link and landed on an internal URL, not your live site

Cause: the Deployment URL and the Domains link are two different things. Fix: on the deployment page, use the link under Domains, not the one under Deployment.

Account problems

GitHub or OpenAI will not connect

Cannot copy the repo, or the authorize screen keeps looping

Cause: your email is not verified, or you got signed out mid-flow. Fix: verify your GitHub email, log back in, and restart the deploy button.

No OpenAI key yet

Fix: go to platform.openai.com/api-keys and click Create new secret key. Copy it the instant it shows, because you cannot view it again later.

Upload problems

Notes count stays at zero, or the upload fails

Note count does not move after uploading

Cause: usually the wrong file was selected, or the zip did not finish compressing before upload. Fix: confirm the .zip file plays nicely by opening it once locally, then try Upload vault again.

Upload rejected

Cause: wrong file type. Fix: the box accepts a vault .zip or individual .md files. Check that you zipped the whole folder and that the files inside are markdown.

Brand Kit problems

Readiness meter will not move, or colors look wrong

Brand readiness stays below 100%

Cause: one or more of the 8 essentials is still empty. Fix: scroll through every card in the Brand kit tab, Identity assets, Typography, Voice DNA, Language guardrails, and Colour system, and confirm each has a value.

Generated visuals do not match my brand colors

Cause: the hex codes in Colour system are placeholders or missing. Fix: replace every swatch with your real hex values, with your main accent color entered first.

Stuck on anything else?

The 30-second rescue loop

  1. Screenshot the whole window, showing what you clicked and the message
  2. Open claude.ai in your browser
  3. Paste the prompt below and attach the screenshot
  4. Follow the numbered steps it gives you. This clears most snags in under a minute
claude.ai · rescue prompt
I'm deploying a web app from a GitHub repo and hit this. Tell me
exactly what to do next: which screen, which button, in numbered
steps. I'm not technical.
Purely Personal

Put Your Brain Online · Live Workshop · Day 2 · 18 July 2026
